query_normalized: the influence of algorithmic recommendations on exploration vs. exploitation in online learning psychology
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (d) by providing a canonical theoretical framework (Active Inference) that explicitly models the mechanism of the exploration-exploitation dilemma in biological and algorithmic agents, which is the core construct of the user's question regarding the psychological underpinnings of these behaviors. Although it focuses on a normative model rather than specific "algorithmic recommendations" in an online platform, it establishes the foundational mechanism for how
